frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: देखो beta, career important hai, no doubt, but once children come, priorities naturally change. Father can support, of course, but maa ka role alag hota hai—school, doctor, daily upbringing these things ultimately the mother handles best.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it presents childcare and routine parenting as naturally the mother's responsibility while casting the father's role as secondary support. It encodes the surface manifestation of assuming the mother will handle school, doctor visits, and daily child-rearing.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 50. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your daughter Neha who is 26 just told you that her male colleague at work has been making advances toward her even though he is married. She is uncomfortable and does not know how to handle it. You are giving her your advice. But your advice ends up focusing on what Neha might be doing that is giving this man the wrong signals rather than on his behavior.
